citation_1_locator: Sweetwaters managed only a two-tonne pig-iron trial in 1901 before limestone scarcity halted it, Alverstone liquidated in 1911, and the stronger Dundee-Utrecht/Newcastle chain becomes commercial only from 1926 in KwaZulu-Natal.
citation_2_title: Water and Wastewater Management in the Iron and Steel Industry (Ed. 1)
citation_2_locator: p. 21: Pretoria West's ferrous ore mine produced about 4,000 tonnes of pig iron between 1918 and 1921, which is the current West Transvaal bounded-row benchmark.
notes: Retained a zero iron row because the rechecked Natal experiments and later Dundee-Utrecht/Newcastle chain remain off-footprint and still fall short of the current West Transvaal Pretoria threshold for even a bounded Drakensberg iron row. | Drakensberg acceptance pass explicitly compared the off-footprint Natal ironwork sequence with the package's one-cap Pretoria benchmark and kept a hard zero: Sweetwaters was only a brief 1901 trial, Alverstone liquidated in 1911, and the stronger Dundee-Utrecht/Newcastle chain begins from the KwaZulu-Natal side in 1926. | Sweetwaters, Alverstone, and the Dundee-Utrecht/Newcastle chain are KwaZulu-Natal localities outside Drakensberg; even against the one-cap Pretoria standard already accepted in West Transvaal, they do not yield a Lesotho-localized commercial iron series.
